chinese_text: 既濟，亨，小利貞，初吉終亂。
translation_text: Ji Ji intimates progress and success in small matters. There will be advantage in being firm and correct. There has been good fortune in the beginning; there may be disorder in the end.
translation_text_raw: Ji Ji intimates progress and success in small matters. There will be advantage in being firm and correct. There has been good fortune in the beginning; there may be disorder in the end.

chinese_text: 初九：曳其輪，濡其尾，无咎。
translation_text: The first NINE, undivided, (shows its subject as a driver) who drags back his wheel, (or as a fox) which has wet his tail. There will be no error.
translation_text_raw: The first NINE, undivided, (shows its subject as a driver) who drags back his wheel, (or as a fox) which has wet his tail. There will be no error.

chinese_text: 六二：婦喪其茀，勿逐，七日得。
translation_text: The second SIX, divided, (shows its subject as) a wife who has lost her (carriage-)screen. There is no occasion to go in pursuit of it. In seven days she will find it.
translation_text_raw: The second SIX, divided, (shows its subject as) a wife who has lost her (carriage-)screen. There is no occasion to go in pursuit of it. In seven days she will find it.

chinese_text: 九三：高宗伐鬼方，三年克之，小人勿用。
translation_text: The third NINE, undivided, (suggests the case of) K?o ?ung, who attacked the Demon region, but was three years in subduing it. Small men should not be employed (in such enterprises).
translation_text_raw: The third NINE, undivided, (suggests the case of) K?o ?ung, who attacked the Demon region, but was three years in subduing it. Small men should not be employed (in such enterprises).

translation_text: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject with rags provided against any leak (in his boat), and on his guard all day long.
translation_text_raw: The fourth SIX, divided, shows its subject with rags provided against any leak (in his boat), and on his guard all day long.

chinese_text: 九五：東鄰殺牛，不如西鄰之禴祭，實受其福。
translation_text: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject (as) the neighbour in the east who slaughters an ox (for his sacrifice); but this is not equal to the (small) spring sacrifice of the neighbour in the west, whose sincerity receives the blessing.
translation_text_raw: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject (as) the neighbour in the east who slaughters an ox (for his sacrifice); but this is not equal to the (small) spring sacrifice of the neighbour in the west, whose sincerity receives the blessing.

translation_text: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject with (even) his head immersed. The position is perilous.
translation_text_raw: The topmost SIX, divided, shows its subject with (even) his head immersed. The position is perilous.
